skill.name: react-best-practices
skill.description: Provides practical React guidance for hooks, effects, refs, custom hooks, and component structure. It helps assistants review and write React components using modern patterns and clear anti-patterns.
skill.summary: Guides React component work with modern hooks, effect, ref, and composition patterns.
skill.author: 0xBigBoss

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command, weak crypto, and reconnaissance indicators, but the cited lines are Markdown prose or TypeScript examples inside SKILL.md. I found no executable scripts, network calls, secret access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent in the reviewed skill content.

content.user_title: Improve React Components with Proven Patterns
content.value_statement: React code often gains bugs from unnecessary effects, unstable dependencies, and unclear component state. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused guidance for safer component design.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use effects for external system synchronization.
Shows alternatives to derived state, effect chains, and prop-change reset effects.
Guides dependency handling with updater functions and effect events.
Documents ref patterns for mutable values, dynamic lists, and imperative handles.
Compares controlled components, composition, context, and focused custom hooks.
limitations: Does not run code, tests, linters, or build tools by itself.
Does not cover every React framework or server component pattern.
Requires project context to choose between data fetching approaches.
Provides guidance only; final code quality depends on implementation review.

use_cases.title: Review React Effects
use_cases.description: Find effects that should be event handlers, derived render values, keys, or memoized calculations.

use_cases.title: Design Reusable Components
use_cases.description: Choose controlled, uncontrolled, composition, context, and ref patterns for predictable component APIs.

use_cases.title: Coach Code Generation
use_cases.description: Steer Claude, Codex, or Claude Code toward modern React hooks and fewer lifecycle-style patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Check One Component
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this React component for effect misuse, derived state, and missing cleanup. Explain the simplest safer pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Hook Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor this component using React best practices. Prefer render calculations, event handlers, keys, and updater functions before effects.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Component API
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this component API for controlled state, composition, context usage, refs, and custom hook boundaries.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a React Migration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a migration plan to reduce effect chains, lifecycle-style hooks, unstable dependencies, and prop drilling across this React module.

output_examples.input: A component stores full name in state and updates it from first and last name changes.
output_examples.output: The review recommends calculating the full name during render because it is derived from existing state.

output_examples.input: A chat room reconnects whenever a theme prop changes.
output_examples.output: The review separates reactive connection inputs from non-reactive notification logic, so theme changes do not reconnect the room.

output_examples.input: A form resets local state with an effect when the selected user changes.
output_examples.output: The review recommends using a keyed child component so React resets state through identity changes.

best_practices: Treat effects as escape hatches for external systems, not general state management.
Fix dependency issues by changing code structure instead of suppressing hook lint rules.
Use refs only for mutable values that should not trigger rendering.
anti_patterns: Do not store values in state when they can be calculated during render.
Do not chain effects to drive application logic that belongs in event handlers.
Do not create lifecycle-style custom hooks that hide missing dependencies.

faq.question: When should this skill be used?
faq.answer: Use it when reviewing or writing React components that use hooks, effects, refs, or custom hooks.

faq.question: Does it replace React documentation?
faq.answer: No. It summarizes practical patterns and should be paired with project requirements and official React guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with TypeScript React code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill explicitly recommends pairing React guidance with TypeScript best practices.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown guidance file and does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: What React problems does it target?
faq.answer: It targets unnecessary effects, unstable dependencies, stale state, ref misuse, prop drilling, and unfocused custom hooks.

faq.question: Is it suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses clear bad and good examples, but users still need basic React knowledge.
